title: Passkeys
drDigitalIntro:
Passkeys let you sign in without typing a password.
• Instead, your device uses Face ID, a fingerprint, or your PIN.
• They're stored on your device and can't be stolen in a data breach.
• More sites — Google, Amazon, Microsoft — are switching to them.

playgroundTask.question: What makes passkeys safer than regular passwords?

options.text: Passkeys are longer than passwords so they're harder to guess
They are stored on your device and use biometrics — they can't be stolen in a data breach
You have to type them twice for extra security

drDigitalSuccess: Passkeys live on your device, not on a server. Even if a website is hacked, there's no password to steal.
drDigitalHint: Think about what makes regular passwords vulnerable — where do they get stolen from?
